title: asclepias viridula
intro: Asclepias viridula, also known as the green antelopehorn milkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Asclepias. This plant is a perennial herb that produces clusters of small, greenish-white flowers and has milky latex sap, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The milky latex sap of Asclepias viridula has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional medicine to stimulate the immune system and promote overall well-being.
